reasoning:
The forecast requires estimating the probability that the daily average temperature at Mont-de-Marsan on three future dates will exceed the temperature on Nov 9. Based on local climatology, the expected average temperature on Nov 9 is roughly 11.2°C. As we move deeper into autumn and winter, the climatological mean drops—reaching about 10°C by Nov 16, 7.5°C by Dec 9, and ~7°C by Feb 7. However, the probability of exceeding the Nov 9 temperature is not simply a function of these means; it depends on the variance and the standard deviation of differences.

- 2025-11-16 (1 week out): The expected mean is about 1°C cooler than Nov 9. Short-term correlation holds the standard deviation of differences to about 5°C, yielding Z ≈ -0.2 and a probability of roughly 41%.
- 2025-12-09 (1 month out): The mean temperature drops significantly (-3.5°C relative to Nov 9). Taking into account seasonal variance and zero short-term correlation, the standard deviation of the difference increases to around 6.3°C. Thus, Z ≈ -0.55, giving a probability near 29%.
- 2026-02-07 (3 months out): February is deep winter in southwest France, with means around 7°C (-4°C relative to Nov 9). Although standard heuristic advice claims uncertainty (and proximity to 50%) should increase with horizon length, the pronounced seasonal drift dominates the probability distribution here. Winter holds larger variance for daily temperatures, giving a standard deviation of differences closer to 6.7°C. Thus, Z ≈ -0.6, giving a probability near 26%.
